name: Petrifying Springs Golf Course
about: Petrifying Springs Golf Course is an 18-hole facility measuring just under 6,000 yards and featuring scenic views, excellent course conditions and an historic clubhouse. Designed by Joseph A. Roseman and opened in 1936, 'Pets' as it is often affectionately called by avid golfers, offers tremendous value and fun. The venue provides a full range of golf services including an on-site golf simulator, practice facilities, instruction, junior golf programs, and a bar and grill for dining.
why_golfers_like_it: Scenic views and excellent course conditions
On-site golf simulator for year-round play

food_and_drink.notes: Full menu available at the on-site Bar & Grill
Full bar available at the on-site Bar & Grill

faq.question: Do I need my own golf clubs?
faq.answer: Players can bring their own clubs or rent from the pro shop. Soft spikes are required and available for purchase at the golf shop.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No, outside food and drinks are not permitted. The venue offers an on-site Bar & Grill with a full menu and bar service.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes on the simulator?
faq.answer: Playing time varies depending on the simulator setup and number of players, typically ranging from 2-4 hours for a full 18 holes.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Petrifying Springs offers a junior golf program and is family-friendly, making it suitable for golfers of all ages.
